excerpts.text: Performs the functions set out in the Corporate Governance Code, in supervisory regulations and in the provisions of laws, regulations, the By-Laws RISK AND SUSTAINABILITY and corporate governance rules in force. In particular, the Committee performs the duties provided for by regulations in force, with functions of COMMITTEE support to the body with strategic supervision powers (e.g. the Board of Directors) in issues involving governance and management of risks and the internal control system, sustainability, and for the approval of periodic financial and non-financial reports. 68 | MONTEPASCHI GROUP 1. Our Vision 2. Our Identity 3. Our Approach 4. Measurement Oil & Gas The Oil & Gas sector, along with coal, is the foundation of global energy production and a major source of greenhouse gas emissions. The scope of Sector companies within this sector ranges from extraction to the sale of derivatives, including refining and transportation. 68 | MONTEPASCHI GROUP 1. Measurement Oil & Gas The Oil & Gas sector, along with coal, is the foundation of global energy production and a major source of greenhouse gas emissions. The scope of Sector companies within this sector ranges from extraction to the sale of derivatives, including refining and transportation. Sectoral scope The scope of analysis includes companies operating in the sectors as follows: • Upstream: B06 - B09 • Core: C19 - C20 • Downstream: D35 - 6. Sectoral scope The scope of analysis includes companies operating in the sectors as follows: • Downstream: D35 - 6. Emissions used Scope 1, 2, 3 Methodology used PCAF PCAF Asset Class Business Loans and unlisted equity PCAF Data Quality* Score 2 Metric Financed emissions Data sources Financial data – internal Emissions – external provider Type of financial data used Corporate loan book –Gross carrying amount exposure Scenario used NGFS NET ZERO 2050 Data coverage Information on emissions at t-0 covers 83% of counterparties and 97.5% of the Gross Carrying Amount of the Oil & Gas sector.

Measurement C&E risks relating to credit risks are, in addition to materiality • for market risk, the market value of non-financial levels that are "very high" (transition risk) and "high" corporate portfolios (bonds and equity) and risk (physical risk), based on potential economic impact (credit exposure to non-collateralised counterparties impairment) that could give rise to adverse climate scenarios. The C&E risks associated with credit risk are, as they are material and highly significant, subject to exposure monitoring as RAS KRIs (at the end of 2023 for the components of private physical risk and transition risk for non-financial companies). • for operational risks, business continuity based on a number of scenario drivers, such as customer hardship The transmission to other “core” risks (market, liquidity and (based on deposit pools), employee hardship (based certain operational risks) of the C&E risk factors analysed was on the number of non-operating employees in the based on what-if analysis, respectively stressing: scenario), operational hardship (based on the number of branches closed), economic damage (based on the loss of profitability for the Bank at risk within the scenario), and physical damage (based on loss of value of owned properties). • for liquidity risk, the liquidity buffers consisting of deposits made by retail customers and non-financial companies, depending on the occurrence of physical risk events concentrated in very short periods of time These risks emerged as immaterial from quarterly verifications and on geographical areas of impact (entire province performed, based on indicators and thresholds suited to for flood risk, individual municipality for landslide risk); capturing changes in the structure of positions and activities deposit run-offs have been assumed both in a situation affected, which could give rise to
